description: Preliminary ID and naming convention for policy packs. This file defines identifiers only, not policy pack content. The policy_pack field remains controlled-open for the current baseline.

id_convention.baseline_application: Do not apply this pattern as a hard schema constraint while policy_pack: open remains valid.

rules.rule: Use lowercase ASCII, digits and hyphens inside segments.
Do not encode policy content or jurisdictional interpretation in the ID beyond framework/domain and profile.
Use a major version suffix to allow later controlled policy-pack evolution.

examples.name: EU AI Act high-risk policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: EU AI Act and high-risk profile are stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

examples.name: HIPAA general policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: HIPAA is stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

examples.name: SOX general policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: SOX is stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

examples.name: ISO/IEC 38507 governance policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: ISO/IEC 38507 is stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

examples.name: IEEE-7000-near ethics policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: IEEE-7000-near ethics checks are stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

examples.name: DSGVO privacy policy pack placeholder
examples.basis: DSGVO-related privacy controls are stated in the Polaris source; pack content remains unspecified.

baseline_compatibility.rule: policy_pack remains a non-empty open string in the current schema baseline.

open_points.point: Concrete policy pack contents and jurisdiction-specific control catalogues are open.
